key_insight: SR-PPO learns token-level credit from a single rollout by training a critic to predict Pass@k reachability, giving a more selective learning signal than Pass@1-based outcomes.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for repeated trajectory sampling in RL for language models, making credit assignment more practical and stable for difficult reasoning tasks.
method: Use one rollout per prompt instead of sampling many contrasting traces
Train a calibrated token-level critic on Monte Carlo outcomes to predict Pass@k at each prefix
Use larger k to approximate reachability and emphasize prefixes that still need learning
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable learning dynamics and consistent gains in Pass@128 success on mathematical reasoning benchmarks, including HMMT26 and AIME24.
watch_item: Pass@k-based credit depends on calibration and may be sensitive to how well the single-rollout Monte Carlo estimates reflect true prefix reachability.
paper_title: Learning with a Single Rollout via Monte Carlo Pass@k Critic
